TABLE II COMPARISON OF ACCURACY PERFORMANCE ON NL4OPT, MAMO (COMPLEXLP/EASYLP), INDUSTRYOR, AND BWOR BENCHMARKS. RESULTS REPORT THE MEAN AND STANDARD DEVIATION (± SD) OVER TEN INDEPENDENT RUNS.

Fig. 6. Population dynamics across generations. The vertical axis denotes training weighted accuracy. Colors indicate the applied evolutionary operators. Red arrows denote elite inheritance. A piecewise scaling is applied to the vertical axis to improve visibility.

Fig. 4. Population-size sensitivity with a fixed iteration depth of T = 8. The training and testing curves report weighted accuracy over ten independent runs, and the shaded regions denote standard deviations. The remaining curves report accuracy on the five evaluation subsets. DeepSeek-v3.2 is used as the evolutionary operator.

Fig. 5. Convergence behavior with a fixed population size of N = 10. The training and testing curves report weighted accuracy over ten independent runs, and the shaded regions denote standard deviations. The remaining curves report accuracy on the five evaluation subsets. DeepSeek-v3.2 is used as the evolutionary operator.
